Hey, what's going on? Okay, so this is phase two of the process. So once you've hit the benchmarks from phase one, you have 50 or more clients on the occurring and you're doing at least 15,000 dollars a month in recurring revenue. That is my recommendation. You know, so you have 15,000 a month of recurring revenue from hybrid is when you might consider going into phase two. Always check with your coach if you want to, if you're not sure. Okay, so essentially what we're doing here is now we're attacking the old model. Okay, and so the old model is the model that right now is exposing you to more risk and not a lot of profit. And so we're trying to essentially just add more revenue from these and clients by adding more services that they deem valuable. Okay, and you'll be able to do this with a position of strength because you now have a an online business that is doing well. And that is why it's prerequisite to doing phase two because you can say in, you know, very truthfully that the people that you have online are getting far better results. You're seeing far more enhancing client experiences, your retention, your lifetime value, your supplement sales, your product sales, all of that stuff are far higher with those clients and they are achieving their goals faster, right? And it's more flexible for them. It's easier. It's on demand, etc. Okay, and so this is all we're doing is we're taking our existing clients and we're bolting on additional revenue for those clients by adding one-on-one accountability to their membership. Okay, and so from an external standpoint, we're still selling. So I'll just want to be clear here. You're still selling from from advertisements to remote to hybrid on the front end. So that has not changed from phase one. That's still consistent. You still always want to be making more period unless you literally can't meet with them because you have so many appointments, which is understandable and you can turn off your ads for, you know, a two week period of time so you can meet with all your clients. It depends on the size of your gym. If you don't have a huge gym, then you can keep both of you going concurrently. If you have a big gym, you have to meet with 200 customers, etc. Then at that point, it might make sense for you to turn off your ads for that period of time and then just focus on bolting on revenue to your existing numbers, which is kind of what this is. So you're taking your large group BFT, which is maybe with my face. You're taking your large group BFT and you're going to basically not convert it, but add something on top of it. Okay, so you're going to run an internal play, which is internal marketing campaign to your existing customers. And then you're going to make them a proposition to add remote training at $200 a month on top of their services. And the reason that you're giving it $200 a month is because that's half of what you're charging cold traffic, which is $400 per month. If there's paying $600, it's $100 a week and you're having and you're going to let your people do it for half off just as a way to honor them for being loyal customers up to this point. And so if you have a large group, this is where you have 200 clients and you try to meet with all of them. If you can, most times you'll be able to meet with three quarters of them and sell about half. And so that's where the goal is to, if you can get 35% of those people onto bolt-on then you are winning. And so you're keeping your main gym the same, but then you're bolting on the hybrid on top of it. That's what we're doing here. So this is just a number example. Let's say if you have 220, you meet with 180 and you sell 100. So this is a crushing example. Normally, I would say if you sell half, maybe 90. Anyways, at $200 a month, the one-on-one coaching on top of the membership. And so that adds $20,000 a month of revenue to the gym without really doing much else, honestly. And that's why this is so great and doesn't really add that much overhead. And then you can also split those clients up between some of the trainers that you have so you can pay them more. And then it makes your entire business more resilient. And then the people that are getting this one-on-one coaching, usually the churn is significantly lower because they are now getting one-on-one attention on a regular basis. They're getting better results. And so you don't have to, you can actually like basically remove them from all the retention processes you normally have at your facility. OK. And so that's where the next benefit is that decreases churn for more because you have one-on-one. Attention, right? And so the beauty here is that you can remove all these clients from your retention system if you already have one in place, which you should. And if you're new to working with us, then you will get a retention system in place for your gym later. But at this point, you wouldn't need to include them in it because now they are being retained by a coach, right? And so one of the nice benefits here is that if you're giving 20%, that's $4,000 a month that's coming in and additional revenue that you're going to pay out and payroll. So you can add $2,000 a month to two of your best trainers and they can make an extra $25,000, $30,000 a year, depending how much you can do 20,000 to 25,000 up to you. And then that way, you really solidify them as full-time because I don't know about you, but whenever I had my trainers, I always wanted to pay them a little bit more than I did. And that way, I wouldn't have to worry about them trying to take side gigs and things like that are leaving, but it actually gives them the security to just have great experience. And you're still taking home, you know, 15,000 plus per month from this process without really having to do the fulfillment. And if you do want to do the fulfillment, then you're more than happy to encourage you to do it if that is your desire. All right. So this is the second phase in the process. So first, we're selling to Cool Traffic. Then once you've had 15,000 plus per month, then you have the conviction that you can give a great service, you can give great experience, you can get people great results. Then you can say with conviction, you can run an internal marketing campaign to your clients to tell them about this new service that you've been offering and basically upgrade as many of the clients that you have to this additional revenue at half price for them as an individual offer to get them to do. And this concludes phase two. Mind you, this might happen four or five months into working with us. Okay. So like be patient. There's a long game here. And maybe you crush your first two months and this happens a week. That's fine. Maybe it happens week 16. That's fine too. All right. It's as long as you're following these steps in process, you can do that your own pace and grow as you desire and make money.
